suggestions: Your deepest flow tends to happen around 4:00 (score 88). Block this time for uninterrupted work.
Strong flow patterns detected. Maintain your current work blocks and keep distractions at bay.

topPatterns.name: No Custom Instructions
topPatterns.description: Only 0% of requests use custom instructions (0/4132). Missing out on personalized responses.
topPatterns.suggestion: Create a .github/copilot-instructions.md or .instructions.md file in your workspace to give Copilot persistent context about your project conventions, stack, and coding style.

topPatterns.name: No Slash Commands
topPatterns.description: Only 0 of 4132 requests use slash commands. Slash commands produce more targeted responses.
topPatterns.suggestion: Try /fix for bugs, /explain for understanding code, /tests for test generation, /doc for documentation.

topPatterns.name: Missing File Context
topPatterns.description: 84% of requests have no file references. Copilot gives better answers with file context.
topPatterns.suggestion: Use #file to reference relevant files, or open files in the editor so Copilot can use them as context.

topPatterns.name: Low Context Provision (Codex)
topPatterns.description: Codex: 3375 requests with context score 15/100. Only 8% include file references, 0% use custom instructions.
topPatterns.suggestion: In Codex, reference files with #file, add .instructions.md files, and use skills/prompts to provide richer context.

topPatterns.name: Prompt Cache Starvation
topPatterns.description: 3249 requests had prompts >5000 tokens but only 9.1% of input tokens came from cache. Each long-prompt request is paying full price for context the model has seen before.
topPatterns.suggestion: Stabilize the front of your prompts: keep custom instructions short and stable, avoid frequent compaction, prefer file references over pasted code, and avoid clearing chat mid-task. Within a session, repeated prefixes get cached and re-used for free.

topPatterns.name: Slow Responses
topPatterns.description: 2772 requests took over 30 seconds (avg 1408s). May indicate overly broad prompts.
topPatterns.suggestion: Break complex tasks into smaller, focused requests. Use lighter models for simple questions.

topPatterns.name: Weekend Overwork
topPatterns.description: 1972 requests (48%) happen on weekends. This may indicate work-life balance issues.
topPatterns.suggestion: Consider maintaining boundaries between work and personal time. Sustained overwork leads to burnout and decreased productivity.

topPatterns.name: Agentic Without Tools
topPatterns.description: 1604 agentic requests used no tools. Agent mode is most effective when tools are enabled.
topPatterns.suggestion: Ensure tools are enabled in agent mode for file search, terminal access, and web search capabilities.

topPatterns.name: Repeated Prompts
topPatterns.description: 1583 requests are near-duplicates across 138 distinct prompts. This wastes quota without new results.
topPatterns.suggestion: If a prompt isn't working, rephrase it or provide more context instead of retrying the same message.

topPatterns.name: Late-Night Coding
topPatterns.description: 1492 requests were made between midnight and 5am. Late-night coding correlates with more bugs and lower quality.
topPatterns.suggestion: Consider establishing healthier work hours. Quality drops significantly when fatigued.
